models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose: This file defines the data model, tests, semantic layer, metrics, and saved queries for order items in a business intelligence context, enabling revenue analysis and reporting by item, order, and time.

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across different database platforms.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized order item metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order cost breakdowns, food/drink item categorization, and customer order history ranking.

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing detailed order information including financial totals, item types, and customer associations. It supports business reporting through semantic models and predefined metrics for order volume, revenue, and customer behavior analysis.

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose: Organizes database schemas by routing seed data to raw schemas and managing custom schema naming conventions for production versus development environments.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to provide detailed information including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply costs for analytical reporting.

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer lifecycle metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metric calculations like average order value.

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that summarizes lifetime purchasing behavior, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose: Provides location data for business sites, supporting geographic analysis and location-based operations management.

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format with cleaned column names and calculated fields. It prepares product information for downstream analytics by renaming columns, converting price units, and adding boolean flags to categorize products.

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose: Establishes a standardized location dimension model with financial metrics for business intelligence, enabling analysis of location performance and tax implications.

models/marts/products.yml.purpose: Defines the product dimension model for analytics, providing standardized product information to support sales and inventory reporting across different business contexts.

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates a surrogate key, and converts cost from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming fields and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the ecom source by renaming columns to standardize field names, specifically mapping 'id' to 'customer_id' and 'name' to 'customer_name'. It serves as a standardized intermediate layer for downstream customer-related analytics and reporting.

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID.

dbt_project.yml.purpose: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It specifies how data models should be materialized and sets up foundational project settings like profile, version requirements, and seed configurations.

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose: Provides a cleaned and transformed list of open locations with basic information, serving as a standardized source for location data used in reporting and analysis.

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose: Defines the supplies dimension table containing supply and product combinations for analytical reporting.

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose: Tracks supply expense records with unique identifiers for each cost entry, supporting financial tracking of supply purchases over time.

models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned customer information with unique identification for each customer record, enabling customer-level analytics and reporting.

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose: Manages product information for food and drink items with unique identifiers, supporting menu management and product-based analytics.

models/marts/products.sql.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis.

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose: Delivers standardized supply chain data to support inventory and supplier management reporting.

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose: Represents individual food and drink items that compose customer orders for transactional analysis.

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose: Stores cleaned and transformed order data representing customer transactions for business analysis.

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose: Configures source data definitions and metadata for raw e-commerce entities including customers, orders, and products.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks for automated code quality checks and formatting. It specifies repositories and hook IDs that enforce YAML validation, whitespace cleanup, dependency file fixes, and Python code linting/formatting.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose: Generates a time spine table containing date information for time-series metric calculations and reporting.

Taskfile.yml.purpose: Defines automated workflows for environment setup, data generation, and database loading operations.

Order Analytics & Revenue.description: Core business domain focused on order processing, revenue analysis, and customer purchasing behavior across the entire order lifecycle.

Product & Supply Chain Management.description: Domain handling product catalog, supply chain data, inventory management, and cost tracking for business operations.

Location & Geographic Operations.description: Domain managing business location data, store operations, and geographic analysis for multi-site business management.

Data Infrastructure & Governance.description: Cross-cutting domain providing foundational data engineering utilities, project configuration, and data governance frameworks.

This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format with cleaned column names and calculated fields. It prepares product information for downstream analytics by renaming columns, converting price units, and adding boolean flags to categorize products.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name) and transformation (cents_to_dollars('price'))
Business rule implementation with coalesce functions to create is_food_item and is_drink_item flags
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming: id → order_id, store_id → location_id, customer → customer_id
Currency conversion from cents to dollars using cents_to_dollars macro
Timestamp truncation using dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at')

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates a surrogate key, and converts cost from cents to dollars while preserving all original supply information.

cents_to_dollars('cost') as supply_cost converts pricing units

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to provide detailed information including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply costs for analytical reporting.

Aggregation of supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_order_items, model.stg_orders, model.stg_products and writes model.order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized order item metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It provides key business insights including order cost breakdowns, food/drink item categorization, and customer order history ranking.

Date truncation function applied to opened_at field to extract day-level granularity
